doc_content.summary: Stark-phase-execute is the autonomous execution engine for complete development phases. It processes a plan slug, automatically decomposes it into tasks if necessary, and executes each task through an end-to-end lifecycle: subagent implementation, PR creation, isolated multi-agent review with autonomous fix rounds, and merging. It concludes with regression testing, releasing, deploying, and generating an observability dashboard with prompt improvement heuristics.
key_features: Full Autonomy: Overrides user-confirmation gates in all downstream sub-workflows.
Auto-Decomposition: Gracefully falls back to invoking /stark-plan-to-tasks if no GH issues exist.
Worktree Isolation: Runs review loops in detached git worktrees to protect primary repository state.
Multi-Agent Feedback Loop: Automatically classifies, fixes, and re-tests review findings up to a configurable max rounds limit.
Dual Authentication: Seamlessly swaps between User PAT for core ops and App Bot Tokens for annotations.
Observability & Tuning: Detects false positive rates and execution bottlenecks to recommend prompt tunings.

alt_text: Architectural flow diagram of the stark-phase-execute skill showing the autonomous pipeline from phase initialization, through a task-by-task implementation and multi-agent review loop, ending with regression testing, deployment, and observability dashboard generation.
